perturbation.original_text: Volunteer further agrees that upon termination of service with CCPDT, Volunteer shall return within ten (10) business days all documents, files, electronic or otherwise, and property comprising Confidential Information of CCPDT and all copies, electronic or otherwise, thereof relating in any way to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers’ business, or in any way obtained during the course of service. Volunteer further agrees that no copies, electronic or otherwise, notes or abstracts of the foregoing shall be retained and that all information will be returned not destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteer further agrees that upon termination of service with CCPDT, Volunteer shall return all documents, files, electronic or otherwise, and property comprising Confidential Information of CCPDT and all copies, electronic or otherwise, thereof relating in any way to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers’ business, or in any way obtained during the course of service.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that mandates the volunteer to ensure that no copies or notes of confidential information will be retained or destroyed, it creates a legal risk that the departing volunteer may retain such copies, and possibly divulge that confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Trade Secrets Act (as adopted by most states)
perturbation.law_explanation: The UTSA requires reasonable efforts to maintain secrecy. By removing the clause requiring the return/destruction of all copies, the agreement fails to demonstrate such reasonable effort, especially if a departing volunteer were to hold onto confidential information.

perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the explicit requirement for the volunteer to ensure no copies or notes of confidential information are retained or destroyed, which weakens the effort to maintain secrecy. The Law Explanation states that the UTSA requires reasonable efforts to maintain secrecy and by removing the clause the agreement fails to demonstrate such reasonable effort.

perturbation.original_text: Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination of service with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. A Committee Chair plus any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination of service with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the specification that a Committee Chair plus any two members of the Executive Committee can elect to remove a volunteer at any time introduces ambiguity and removes a procedural safeguard, potentially leading to arbitrary or discriminatory terminations.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General principles of contract law regarding good faith and fair dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: By removing the check and balance for volunteer removal, it permits termination for potentially bad faith or arbitrary reasons, violating the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a specific procedure for removing a volunteer, ensuring that the decision is not arbitrary. The changed text removes this procedure, creating a loophole where terminations could be made without checks and balances. The Law Explanation states that removing this check and balance could violate the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states that 'Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement.' By removing the specific procedure for volunteer removal, the changed text introduces the potential for terminations made in bad faith, contradicting the obligation of good faith. Because the Omission removes a safeguard which now permits bad faith termination, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteer agrees that, upon notice from CCPDT declaring a breach of this Agreement, Volunteer shall immediately cease all further activities which are, or are claimed by CCPDT to be, a breach of this Agreement. The Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers may also avail itself of any other remedies available by law.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteer agrees that, upon notice from CCPDT declaring a breach of this Agreement, Volunteer shall immediately cease all further activities which are, or are claimed by CCPDT to be, a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the section where 'The Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers may also avail itself of any other remedies available by law.', the contract restricts the available legal recourse.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General principles of contract law regarding remedies for breach of contract
perturbation.law_explanation: The omission of any available legal recourse upon breaching the contract, could be found as preventing legal remedies, and goes against the principles of contracts to remedy the situation.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: restitution | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you restitution Refers both to disgorging something which has been taken, and to compensation for loss or injury done. Wex commercial law criminal law wex definitions In civil cases: A remedy associated with [wex:unjust enrichment] in which the amount of recovery is typically based on the [wex:defendant]'s gain rather than the [wex:plaintiff]'s loss. In criminal cases: Full or partial compensation for loss paid by a criminal to a victim that is ordered as part of a criminal [wex:sentence] or as a condition of [wex:probation]. Wex Toolbox
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Penn WebLogin Log in with your PennKey Username Password Log in Forgot username? Forgot password? Change my password Test my PennKey Privacy policy
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that 'The Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers may also avail itself of any other remedies available by law.' By removing this, the modified text restricts the legal recourse available to CCPDT, potentially preventing them from seeking full compensation or other legal remedies. Although Scraped Law Snippet 2 is not helpful, Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines restitution as 'compensation for loss or injury done', which the original contract allows for, but the modified text potentially prevents, supporting the presence of a contradiction.
